description_long: This helmet is a cylindrical, medieval-style great helm made primarily of dark, mottled metal with a weathered texture. The front features a prominent vertical brass or gold-colored reinforcement strip, flanked by a horizontal band that frames two narrow eye slits for vision. The lower front section is perforated with a grid of small round ventilation holes, enhancing breathability while maintaining protection. The helmet is constructed from riveted metal plates, with visible rivets distributed evenly around the crown and sides. The back and sides are plain, lacking any decorative elements or additional features. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, evoking historical armor used by knights or soldiers.
description: A cylindrical, medieval great helm made of dark, weathered metal with brass accents, featuring eye slits and front ventilation holes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the brass vertical and horizontal bands, eye slits, and perforated lower faceplate.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the curved profile, rivets, and partial brass band visible near the front.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, riveted metal surface with no openings or decorations.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the helmet's cylindrical shape and rivet pattern.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: Multiple small round holes arranged in a grid pattern on the lower front faceplate.
